title: Expose ontology-aware runtime input/status CLI for external supervisors
description:
## Context

`imm_supervisor` currently decides Codex/OMX state by scraping tmux pane text and injects prompts with local tmux logic. This creates false busy/idle decisions (for example, stale `Ran` lines below a now-idle `›` prompt) and duplicates ccbot runtime-input behavior.

The desired direction is to route both runtime input and runtime state through ccbot core, using ccbot ontology/control-plane semantics instead of ad-hoc supervisor scraping.

Important boundary:

- `ccbot send` is Telegram delivery surface (`ccbot send --help` now exposes that CLI) and must remain text/file delivery to Telegram.
- Runtime/TUI input needs a separate ontology-aware command/API, not `ccbot send`.

## Proposed work

Add a ccbot core runtime-control CLI/API for external supervisors, e.g. `ccbot inject`, `ccbot input`, or `ccbot runtime-send`, plus a state/status query if one does not already exist.

The command/API should:

- resolve the target through existing ccbot state/ontology where possible (`CCBOT_DIR`, surface/window binding, runtime kind, tmux window id);
- use existing live runtime input-plane checks and fail closed when no writable runtime plane is attached;
- reuse existing `RuntimeInputDriver` / `TmuxManager` paths, including Codex multiline `paste-buffer -p -d` + bare `Enter` semantics;
- provide machine-readable output suitable for supervisors (`--json`), including status such as `input_ready`, `busy`, `blocked_prompt`, `read_only`, `no_live_input_plane`, and target/window metadata;
- keep `ccbot send` documented and tested as Telegram delivery, not runtime injection.

## imm_supervisor consumer need

After this lands, `/home/tools/imm/scripts/imm_tmux_supervisor.sh` should stop scraping pane tails for state and stop embedding tmux prompt delivery. It should ask ccbot for runtime state and call the new ccbot runtime-input command when ccbot says the target is writable/input-ready.

## Acceptance criteria

- New ccbot runtime-input/status command or documented API exists and has `--help`.
- `ccbot send --help` remains Telegram delivery help and tests prove it is not runtime injection.
- Runtime input path uses ccbot ontology/live-input-plane guardrails and fails closed on read-only/no live plane.
- Codex multiline prompts use existing paste-buffer + bare Enter behavior through ccbot, not duplicated supervisor code.
- Tests cover CLI contract and at least one no-live-plane/read-only failure.
- Docs/ontology mention the boundary between delivery surface (`ccbot send`) and runtime input plane.

title: Ignore image ingress in unbound Telegram topics
description: If a Telegram topic/thread is not in the active bound runtime state, image-equivalent ingress should be silent instead of producing bind/unbound guidance or other visible bot reactions. Scope: incoming photo_handler images and sticker_handler image-equivalent stickers/thumbnails. Do not change ccbot send, runtime-input, inject, text, or command bind flows.
acceptance_criteria: Unbound/non-bind photo messages do not download files, do not call session_manager.send_to_window, do not call safe_reply, and do not open/mutate bind flow. Unbound/non-bind sticker messages follow the same silent behavior. Bound photo/sticker delivery behavior remains unchanged. Regression tests cover unbound photo and sticker ingress.
notes:
GitHub issue https://github.com/six-ddc/ccbot/issues/84 was created before the request was clarified to Beads; keep the canonical task here.
Work started 2026-05-02 on p2 /home/tools/ccbot feat/runtime-input-cli. Requirements source of truth is the Bead text; no deploy/restart planned.
Implemented and pushed in 08a7136. Verification: 114 passed in tests/ccbot/test_bot_contracts.py; 23 passed in tests/ccbot/test_docs_contracts.py; targeted ruff clean; full pytest 735 passed; desloppify scan/status/next strict 79.0, queue 0, no open findings for telegram_delivery_policy.py or touched tests. Live deploy not run by request.
close_reason: Acceptance verified and implementation pushed in 08a7136; unbound photo/sticker ingress is silent with no download, reply, runtime send, or bind mutation.

title: Generated image success text is not delivered back to Telegram chat
description:
## Correction
This issue is not about delivering the generated image as a Telegram attachment. The expected outcome was a text success/result message in the Telegram chat after the image generation completed.

## Problem
A coding agent generated an improved sticker/image artifact with `gpt-image-2`, and the runtime showed a successful generation result plus a local file path, but the originating Telegram chat did not receive the final text message reporting success/result/path.

Observed example from @ImmArenaBot flow:
- Tool output reported: `Generated Image`
- Saved file: `/home/tools/imm/.codex/generated_images/019de621-9462-7bd0-b2b6-b0b5fe814107/ig_07505510d95b59320169f5ace6313881919be01593ef043a65.png`
- The user expected Telegram text delivery of the success/result message, not an automatic image attachment.
- No such Telegram text result arrived.

## Expected behavior
When the runtime produces a final user-facing success/result message after image generation, compact Telegram delivery should deliver that final text to the bound/originating chat/thread. The text may include the generated file path or a concise result note.

## Actual behavior
The success/result is visible in the runtime transcript, but the Telegram surface does not receive the final text message.

## Boundaries
- Do not require automatic image attachment delivery for this bug.
- Do not repurpose `ccbot send`; it remains outbound Telegram delivery.
- Do not change runtime/TUI input semantics (`runtime-input`/`inject`).
- Preserve topic/thread binding, compact final-message semantics, and CCBOT_DIR resolution guardrails.
- Do not print or expose Telegram tokens/secrets in diagnostics.

## Initial hypothesis
This is likely a compact delivery/final-message detection or routing gap for generated-image success output in the IMM-controlled runtime, not a missing media attachment feature.
acceptance_criteria: After a generated-image operation completes in an IMM/ccbot runtime, the originating Telegram thread receives the final text success/result message; no automatic image attachment is required for this bug; tests/docs cover the compact delivery contract.
notes:
Work started 2026-05-02 on p2 /home/tools/ccbot feat/runtime-input-cli. Requirements source of truth is the Bead text; no deploy/restart planned.
Implemented and pushed in 08a7136. Verification: 114 passed in tests/ccbot/test_bot_contracts.py; 23 passed in tests/ccbot/test_docs_contracts.py; targeted ruff clean; full pytest 735 passed; desloppify scan/status/next strict 79.0, queue 0, no open findings for telegram_delivery_policy.py or touched tests. Live deploy not run by request.
close_reason: Acceptance verified and implementation pushed in 08a7136; generated-image success text now becomes compact terminal Telegram text without automatic media attachment.

title: Support artifact-first inbound Telegram audio/video media ingress
description:
Implement artifact-first inbound Telegram audio/video media ingress for IMM/ccbot.

Context:
- OpenAI keys for this account are unavailable/deactivated, so MVP must not require OpenAI transcription.
- Local OSS ASR/diarization artifacts may exist in the IMM environment (`/home/tools/imm/.venv-voice`), but they are future optional enrichment and must not become a ccbot runtime dependency now.
- `ccbot send` remains outbound Telegram delivery; `runtime-input`/`inject` remain runtime/TUI input CLIs.
- Planning artifacts:
  - .omx/plans/prd-audio-video-ingress-20260503T084259Z.md
  - .omx/plans/test-spec-audio-video-ingress-20260503T084259Z.md
  - .omx/plans/ontology-audio-video-ingress-20260503T084259Z.md
acceptance_criteria:
- Bound Telegram audio messages are saved under a safe local media artifact path and forwarded to the runtime with `Audio artifact received.`, `Audio artifact: ...`, metadata, and `Transcript: unavailable (...)` when no valid backend exists.
- Bound Telegram video messages are saved under a safe local media artifact path and forwarded with `Video artifact received.`, `Video artifact: ...`, metadata, and optional thumbnail image marker; if preview is unavailable, runtime text includes `Preview unavailable` and still sends the original artifact path.
- Missing/invalid/deactivated OpenAI credentials do not block artifact delivery and do not trigger OpenAI transcription calls.
- Unbound group-topic audio/video ingress is silent: no download, no safe_reply, no send_to_window, no bind mutation.
- Size/mime/path guardrails run before download where Telegram metadata allows; unsafe/oversize/download failure in a bound topic fails closed with a clear warning.
- Existing voice/photo/document/sticker behavior remains compatible; `ccbot send`, `runtime-input`, and `inject` semantics are unchanged.
- Tests/docs/ontology are updated and targeted pytest + ruff checks pass before deploy.
notes:
Started artifact-first audio/video ingress implementation in p2 /home/tools/ccbot. Deep-interview sanity: no user question needed; ontology checkpoint: audio/video are media artifacts, transcript/preview optional enrichment, ccbot send remains outbound delivery, local OSS ASR remains future work.
Implementation complete on p2 pending deploy. Verification: tests/ccbot/test_bot_contracts.py + test_docs_contracts.py passed (143); full pytest passed (741); ruff check src/ccbot tests/ccbot passed; ccbot send --help still exposes outbound --file-path/--file-type. Review: guardrails resolve bound target before download with silent_unbound=True; audio/video do not call OpenAI; video preview is non-fatal; ccbot send/runtime-input/inject semantics unchanged. Desloppify scan ran; queue clear, strict 79.0, unrelated backlog remains.
close_reason: Implemented in b89850f and deployed to str. Verification: full pytest 741 passed, bot contracts/docs 143 passed, ruff src/ccbot tests/ccbot passed, ccbot send help unchanged. str /tools/ccbot HEAD b89850f; imported audio_handler/video_handler; ccbot.service PID 1854265 and imm_arena_bot.service PID 1854287 active since 2026-05-03 10:18:40 UTC; journals since restart show no errors/timeouts.

title: Continue ccbot desloppify cleanup beyond current small batch
description: Follow-up from 2026-05-02 cleanup pass. Current desloppify status after commits: strict 79.0/100, 251 open, focus Cross-module arch 68.0, queue/planned review work remains. Top remaining items include MessageTask variant bag, SessionManager state hub, callback window authorization binding checks, delivery package placement, and audit API width. Keep future batches small, behavior-locked with tests, and avoid mixing production behavior fixes with broad cleanup.
acceptance_criteria: Run desloppify next/show review, choose one bounded item, add/keep regression coverage, run targeted tests plus ruff, rescan, and record resolved issues with desloppify plan commit-log.
